findings.description: IDOR: complete_day() accepts plan_id from URL without verifying it belongs to the authenticated user. Any authenticated user can mark any other user's plan day as complete.
findings.required_fix:
Add ownership check before mark_day_complete: query the workout_plans table for plan_id and verify user_id matches. Return 403 if mismatch: 'plan_id not found for this user'. Example:
    plan = db.execute('SELECT id FROM workout_plans WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?', (plan_id, user['id'])).fetchone()
    if not plan:
        return jsonify({'error': 'Plan not found'}), 403
findings.resolution: Fixed in e944d96: ownership check added

findings.description: complete_day() logs session with duration_minutes=0 hardcoded, but acceptance criteria says 'Mark as Done → logs session automatically' implying a meaningful duration.
findings.evidence: log_training_session(user["id"], date_str=today, duration_minutes=0, notes=f"Plan {plan_id} day {day_idx} completed")
findings.required_fix: Either: (1) use the timerSeconds value from the active timer to compute actual duration, or (2) query estimated_minutes from planned_exercises for this day and use that. Currently always logs 0 minutes which is misleading.

findings.description: No test coverage for workout_plan model or plan routes. The new routes/plan.py and models/workout_plan.py have zero test coverage.
findings.evidence: tests/test_models.py contains only TestNutritionModel, TestWeightLog, TestSleepLog, TestUserModel. No TestWorkoutPlan or test routes for /training-program endpoints.
findings.required_fix: Add tests for: (1) create_workout_plan/get_active_plan round-trip, (2) mark_day_complete, (3) generate_plan endpoint (mock AI), (4) current_plan endpoint, (5) complete_day with ownership check (positive and negative cases).
